title: Storyboard · "Knock" — Radio→Animation production script · S15
body:
**Scene 15: 18:30–19:30 · Walter Pours**

HERO FRAME: A small parlor at night · single brass lamp lit · a half-empty glass of dark liquid on the side table · an open prescription bottle next to the glass · a folded sheet of writing paper with "TO BE FOUND" written across the top · Walter's hand visible holding the cap

MOTION: kling — the lamplight pulses gently. Walter's hand trembles. The glass surface ripples. The folded note slowly unfolds at one corner from a draft. **The room is too still.**

MOOD: The deepest part of the story. Held with reverence — never graphic.

AUDIO: Walter (quieter, more strained): *"Tonight is the night I was going to stop. I had decided. I had a bottle. I had a list. I had a letter. I had it all ready."*

source.label: Dimension X episode #05 (NBC Radio, May 6, 1950) adapting Fredric Brown's 1948 short-short story "Knock" (originally in *Thrilling Wonder Stories*, December 1948)

author: matt

witnesses.label: Rufinus of Aquileia, Commentary on the Apostles' Creed (c. AD 404)
Pirminius, De singulis libris canonicis scarapsus (c. AD 750) — earliest received form
Cited by every major catechism since 8th century
